_publications._publicationTitle: Transient inhibition of the ERK pathway prevents cerebellar developmental defects and improves long-term motor functions in murine models of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: The MEKi-treated Nf1 hGFAP CKO mice stayed significantly longer on the Rotarod compared to vehicle-treated mutant mice... the MEKi-treated mutant mice exhibited a significant improvement similar to controls
details: MEKi-treated Nf1 hGFAP CKO mice stayed significantly longer on Rotarod compared to vehicle-treated mutant mice and exhibited significant improvement in motor learning similar to controls
